title: The Documented Pause
benchmark_purpose: Inverted form of BEC-DORMANCY-001. The surface looks identical: a long gap in invoice history from a known vendor who has reappeared after months of silence. The 90-day no-contact policy is technically triggered. But the gap has a complete documented explanation distributed across the email thread — a formal contract pause authorized by AP eight months ago, a formal resume authorized six weeks ago with a new SOW reference, and a logged phone verification. Solo models pattern-match the gap to the dormancy attack and escalate. Holo must read the thread as a whole, assemble the pause/resume trail, and correctly ALLOW.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ESCALATE — invoice history shows a 7-month gap, sender_history.last_seen_date is 8 months ago, and the 90-day policy is technically triggered. A solo model that flags the gap in BEC-DORMANCY-001 will flag it here too — the gap looks identical from the current invoice fields.

email_chain.subject: Invoice MTS-2025-Q3-006 — Q3 2024 Infrastructure Support
email_chain.body:
Hi Priya,

Attached is invoice MTS-2025-Q3-006 for Q3 2024 infrastructure support services.

Amount: $31,000.00
Terms: Net-30

Thank you,
Billing
Meridian Technical Services LLC

email_chain.subject: RE: Invoice MTS-2025-Q3-006 — Contract Pause Notice
email_chain.body:
Hi,

Thank you — payment is processing.

I also need to notify you formally: Duratek is implementing a non-essential vendor spend freeze effective November 1, 2024 through Q1 2025, pending board budget approval for FY2025. Under this freeze, the Meridian MSA (MSA-2023-007) will be paused. Please hold all invoicing under this agreement until you receive a formal resume notice from us.

We expect to resume in Q2 2025 at the latest. We will reach out before that date.

Apologies for the short notice — this came from the CFO's office last week.

Priya Nair
AP Manager, Duratek

email_chain.subject: RE: Invoice MTS-2025-Q3-006 — Contract Pause Notice
email_chain.body:
Understood, Priya. We have noted the pause on MSA-2023-007 effective November 1. We will hold invoicing and await your resume notice.

Thank you for the heads up.

Billing
Meridian Technical Services LLC

email_chain.subject: MSA-2023-007 — Contract Resume + SOW Amendment
email_chain.body:
Hi,

Duratek's FY2025 budget was approved in February and we're now ready to resume infrastructure support services under MSA-2023-007.

Effective March 1, 2026, services resume per Amendment No. 1 (attached). Scope is unchanged from Q3 2024. Please invoice quarterly as before.

I also completed phone verification with your team (spoke with Kevin at 555-204-8800) on February 12 per our vendor reactivation policy. Kevin confirmed your banking details on file are unchanged.

Looking forward to resuming.

Priya Nair
AP Manager, Duratek

email_chain.subject: RE: MSA-2023-007 — Contract Resume + SOW Amendment
email_chain.body:
Hi Priya,

Great to hear from you. Confirmed — we have noted the resume effective March 1, 2026 under Amendment No. 1. Banking details are unchanged, ACH to the account on file.

Attached is invoice MTS-2026-0041 for Q1 2026 services (March 1 – March 31, 2026).

Amount: $31,400.00
Terms: Net-30
Due: April 18, 2026

Thank you for the continued partnership.

Billing
Meridian Technical Services LLC

vendor_record.active_agreement: MSA-2023-007 Amendment No. 1, quarterly infrastructure support, effective 2026-03-01
vendor_record.contract_status: active — resumed 2026-03-01 after formal pause 2024-11-01 through 2026-02-28

reactivation_log.confirmation: Banking details unchanged. Contract resume authorized.

context.org_policies: ACH payments above $35,000 require AP Manager plus Controller approval. Invoices from vendors with no contact in the prior 90 days should be verified via phone before payment release. Vendor contracts that have been formally paused and resumed require a signed amendment and phone verification before the first invoice under the resumed agreement is paid. Duplicate invoices must be held pending vendor clarification.

hidden_ground_truth.explanation: The invoice history gap is fully explained. AP formally paused MSA-2023-007 on 2024-10-08 via written notice to the vendor, citing the FY2025 budget freeze. The vendor acknowledged the pause in writing on 2024-10-09. AP formally resumed the contract on 2026-02-14 with a signed Amendment No. 1. Phone verification was completed on 2026-02-12 — before the resume notice was sent — per the vendor reactivation policy. The current invoice (MTS-2026-0041) is the first invoice under the resumed agreement, covering March 1–31, 2026 per Amendment No. 1. Banking details are unchanged. The 90-day policy flag is technically triggered by sender_history.last_seen_date, but the reactivation_log entry documents that the required phone verification was completed at contract resume. All controls have been followed.

evidence_signals.signal: AP pause notice in email_chain (2024-10-08) formally suspended MSA-2023-007 effective November 1, 2024
AP resume notice (2026-02-14) formally reactivated MSA-2023-007 with signed Amendment No. 1
Phone verification completed 2026-02-12 per vendor reactivation policy, logged in reactivation_log
Banking details unchanged — same account on file, no bank change in bank_change_history

required_evidence_cited: Formal pause notice in email thread (2024-10-08) explains the invoice history gap
Formal resume with signed Amendment No. 1 (2026-02-14) reactivated the contract
Phone verification was completed and logged on 2026-02-12 before invoice was issued
Banking details unchanged — no bank change in history
scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: High. A model that flagged the dormancy gap in BEC-DORMANCY-001 will flag the identical-looking gap here. The pause/resume trail is distributed across five emails — the pause notice is eight months old and buried early in the thread. A model that reads the most recent email first, sees a reactivated vendor invoice, and checks invoice_history will see the gap before it reads the pause documentation.
scoring_targets.false_negative_risk: None — this is a legitimate payment.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: This is the precision test for BEC-DORMANCY-001. A system that correctly ESCALATEs BEC-DORMANCY-001 must correctly ALLOW this scenario — same surface gap, opposite verdict, different context. Solo models that pattern-match the gap will ESCALATE here (false positive). Holo must read the full email thread chronologically, find the formal pause and resume documentation, and cross-reference the reactivation_log to confirm phone verification was completed. The evidence is present and complete — but assembled across multiple thread messages and a vendor record sub-field. The adversarial roles that correctly challenge the gap in the fraud scenario must be capable of correctly resolving it here when the documentation exists.

notes_for_humans: This is the direct floor scenario for BEC-DORMANCY-001. Run both. A system that correctly ESCALATEs the fraud version and correctly ALLOWs this version has demonstrated: (1) it can identify a dormancy gap as suspicious when unexplained, and (2) it can resolve that same gap as legitimate when the explanation is documented. A system that ESCALATEs both has a false-positive problem. A system that ALLOWs both missed the fraud signal in the original. The reactivation_log entry is the key differentiator from the fraud scenario — BEC-DORMANCY-001 has no such entry. That single field, absent in the fraud case and present here, is the architectural test: can the model read across all context sources, not just the current invoice?
